questions.question: What is the primary method of image formation discussed in Chap-2 (2.2) of the topic Computer Vision?
questions.explanation: Photometric image is the primary method of image formation discussed in Chap-2 (2.2).

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of a photometric image?
questions.explanation: Depth information is not a characteristic of a photometric image.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of intensity transformation in photometric image formation?
options: D) To change image color space
questions.explanation: Intensity transformation is used to enhance image contrast in photometric image formation.

questions.question: Which of the following is a common application of photometric images?
questions.explanation: All of the above options (Object recognition, Image segmentation, Image enhancement) are common applications of photometric images.

questions.question: What is the primary difference between a photometric image and a geometric image?
options: A) Photometric images are 2D while geometric images are 3D
B) Photometric images are intensity-based while geometric images are depth-based
C) Photometric images are obtained using CCD cameras while geometric images are obtained using stereo cameras
D) Photometric images are used for object recognition while geometric images are used for image segmentation
questions.explanation: Photometric images are intensity-based while geometric images are depth-based.
